name: Mount Meru
shortDescription: Mount Meru is the sacred mountain in Hindu cosmology, considered the center of the universe and the abode of deities.
description: Mount Meru, also known as Sumeru, is the central mountain in Hindu, Jain, and Buddhist cosmology. It is described as a towering, golden mountain, the axis mundi of the universe, around which all other realms revolve. In Hindu mythology, Mount Meru is the residence of deities like Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva, although they may have separate abodes within or near it. It is the meeting point between heaven and earth. The mountain is described as being surrounded by celestial cities, oceans, and continents. Its slopes are adorned with precious stones and jewels, and it is the source of life-giving rivers. The sun and moon revolve around Mount Meru, illuminating the cosmos. During the Samudra Manthana (churning of the ocean), Mount Meru served as the churning rod, supported by Vishnu in his Kurma avatar. Its significance extends beyond a physical location; it symbolizes spiritual aspiration, the pursuit of enlightenment, and the connection between the earthly and the divine. The mountain’s stability and grandeur represent the steadfastness of dharma and the unchanging nature of the ultimate reality.

location.cosmological: Center of the universe, axis mundi in Hindu cosmology, located in the mythical realm of Jambudvipa.
